forecast-period.text: A series of low pressure systems passing through the Bight will extend cold fronts that will cross over SA through to the end of the week. A high pressure system is then forecast to move into the Bight from late Friday and extend a ridge across the state over the weekend.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Wednesday for Far West Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 25 to 30 knots turning northwesterly in the evening.
2.5 to 3 metres.
West to southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres by early evening.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers offshore, 50% chance elsewhere.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Far West Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 25 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 15 to 25 knots during the morning and early afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 5 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning south to southeasterly below 10 knots during the morning.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 4 to 5 metres.
Cloudy.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Wednesday for Upper West Coast
forecast-period.text: West to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots south of Streaky Bay in the late evening.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2 to 3 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for Upper West Coast
forecast-period.text: West to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots south of Streaky Bay early in the morning. Winds tending west to southwesterly 20 to 30 knots during the morning and early afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2 to 4 metres, tending southwesterly 4 to 6 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots becoming variable about 10 knots during the day.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 4 to 6 metres, decreasing to 4 metres during the evening.
Cloudy.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Wednesday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: West to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots in the late evening.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres, tending westerly 2.5 to 3 metres during the afternoon, then increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late morning.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: West to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots early in the morning. Winds tending west to southwesterly during the morning and early afternoon.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Westerly 3 to 5 metres, tending southwesterly 5 to 6 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm inshore in the early morning.
Southwesterly 20 to 30 knots decreasing to 15 to 20 knots during the morning then becoming southerly about 10 knots during the afternoon.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 5 to 6 metres, decreasing to 4 metres during the evening.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Wednesday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: West to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots in the late evening.
2.5 to 3 metres.
West to southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 4 metres in the west during the afternoon.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late morning.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: West to northwesterly 30 to 35 knots becoming westerly 25 to 30 knots before dawn then increasing to 25 to 35 knots in the middle of the day.
3 metres.
Westerly 4 to 6 metres, tending southwesterly 6 to 7 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Southwesterly 20 to 30 knots decreasing to about 10 knots during the evening.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 5 to 7 metres, decreasing to 4 to 5 metres during the evening.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.
